   583→	SuppressedReasonSilence            = "silence"
   584→	SuppressedReasonInhibition         = "inhibition"
   585→	SuppressedReasonMuteTimeInterval   = "mute_time_interval"
   586→	SuppressedReasonActiveTimeInterval = "active_time_interval"
   587→)
   588→
   589→// WaitStage waits for a certain amount of time before continuing or until the
   590→// context is done.
   591→type WaitStage struct {
   592→	wait func() time.Duration
   593→}
   594→
   595→// NewWaitStage returns a new WaitStage.
   596→func NewWaitStage(wait func() time.Duration) *WaitStage {
   597→	return &WaitStage{
   598→		wait: wait,
   599→	}
   600→}
   601→
   602→// Exec implements the Stage interface.
   603→func (ws *WaitStage) Exec(ctx context.Context, _ *slog.Logger, alerts ...*types.Alert) (context.Context, []*types.Alert, error) {
   604→	select {
   605→	case <-time.After(ws.wait()):
   606→	case <-ctx.Done():
   607→		return ctx, nil, ctx.Err()
   608→	}
   609→	return ctx, alerts, nil
   610→}

   618→
   619→	now  func() time.Time

sync.Mutex\n\t\terrs error\n\t)\n\twg.Add(len(fs))\n\n\tfor _, s := range fs {\n\t\tgo func(s Stage) {\n\t\t\tif _, _, err := s.Exec(ctx, l, alerts...); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tmtx.Lock()\n\t\t\t\terrs = errors.Join(errs, err)\n\t\t\t\tmtx.Unlock()\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\twg.Done()\n\t\t}(s)\n\t}\n\twg.Wait()\n\n\treturn ctx, alerts, errs\n}\n\n// GossipSettleStage waits until the Gossip has settled to forward alerts.\ntype GossipSettleStage struct {\n\tpeer Peer\n}\n\n// NewGossipSettleStage returns a new GossipSettleStage.\nfunc NewGossipSettleStage(p Peer) *GossipSettleStage {\n\treturn &GossipSettleStage{peer: p}\n}\n\nfunc (n *GossipSettleStage) Exec(ctx context.Context, _ *slog.Logger, alerts ...*types.Alert) (context.Context, []*types.Alert, error) {\n\tif n.peer != nil {\n\t\tif err := n.peer.WaitReady(ctx); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn ctx, nil, err\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn ctx, alerts, nil\n}\n\nconst (\n\tSuppressedReasonSilence            = \"silence\"\n\tSuppressedReasonInhibition         = \"inhibition\"\n\tSuppressedReasonMuteTimeInterval   = \"mute_time_interval\"\n\tSuppressedReasonActiveTimeInterval = \"active_time_interval\"\n)\n\n// WaitStage waits for a certain amount of time before continuing or until the\n// context is done.\ntype WaitStage struct {\n\twait func() time.Duration\n}\n\n// NewWaitStage returns a new WaitStage.\nfunc NewWaitStage(wait func() time.Duration) *WaitStage {\n\treturn &WaitStage{\n\t\twait: wait,\n\t}\n}\n\n// Exec implements the Stage interface.\nfunc (ws *WaitStage) Exec(ctx context.Context, _ *slog.Logger, alerts ...*types.Alert) (context.Context, []*types.Alert, error) {\n\tselect {\n\tcase <-time.After(ws.wait()):\n\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n\t\treturn ctx, nil, ctx.Err()\n\t}\n\treturn ctx, alerts, nil\n}\n\n// DedupStage filters alerts.\n// Filtering happens based on a notification log.\ntype DedupStage struct {\n\trs    ResolvedSender\n\tnflog    638→// Wrap a slice in a struct so we can store a pointer in sync.Pool.
   639→type hashBuffer struct {
   640→	buf []byte
   641→}

   643→var hashBuffers = sync.Pool{

   645→}

   647→func hashAlert(a *types.Alert) uint64 {
   648→	const sep = '\xff'
   649→
   650→	hb := hashBuffers.Get().(*hashBuffer)
   651→	defer hashBuffers.Put(hb)
   652→	b := hb.buf[:0]
   653→
   654→	names := make(model.LabelNames, 0, len(a.Labels))
   655→
   656→	for ln := range a.Labels {
   657→		names = append(names, ln)
   658→	}
   659→	sort.Sort(names)
   660→
   661→	for _, ln := range names {
   662→		b = append(b, string(ln)...)
   663→		b = append(b, sep)
   664→		b = append(b, string(a.Labels[ln])...)
   665→		b = append(b, sep)
   666→	}
   667→
   668→	hash := xxhash.Sum64(b)
   669→
   670→	return hash
   671→}

   678→	ReasonNewAlertsInGroup
   679→	ReasonNewResolvedAlerts
   680→	ReasonAllAlertsResolved
   681→	ReasonRepeatIntervalElapsed
   682→	ReasonUnknown

   689→func (r NotifyReason) String() string {
   690→	switch r {
   691→	case ReasonDoNotNotify:
   692→		return "none"
   693→	case ReasonFirstNotification:
   694→		return "first notification"
   695→	case ReasonNewAlertsInGroup:
   696→		return "new alerts added"
   697→	case ReasonNewResolvedAlerts:
   698→		return "some alerts resolved"
   699→	case ReasonAllAlertsResolved:
   700→		return "all alerts resolved"
   701→	case ReasonRepeatIntervalElapsed:
   702→		return "repeat interval elapsed"

   708→func (n *DedupStage) needsUpdate(entry *nflogpb.Entry, firing, resolved map[uint64]struct{}, repeat time.Duration) NotifyReason {
   709→	// If we haven't notified about the alert group before, notify right away
   710→	// unless we only have resolved alerts.
   711→	if entry == nil {
   712→		if len(firing) > 0 {
   713→			return ReasonFirstNotification
   714→		}
   715→		return ReasonDoNotNotify
   716→	}
   717→
   718→	// new alerts in the group
   719→	if !entry.IsFiringSubset(firing) {
   720→		// If the previous entry has no firing alerts, it was a resolution and we
   721→		// should treat this as the first notification for the group.
   722→		if len(entry.FiringAlerts) == 0 {
   723→			return ReasonFirstNotification
   724→		}
   725→		return ReasonNewAlertsInGroup
   726→	}
   727→
   728→	// Notify about all alerts being resolved.
   729→	// This is done irrespective of the send_resolved flag to make sure that
   730→	// the firing alerts are cleared from the notification log.
   731→	if len(firing) == 0 {
   732→		// If the current alert group and last notification contain no firing
   733→		// alert, it means that some alerts have been fired and resolved during the
   734→		// last interval. In this case, there is no need to notify the receiver
   735→		// since it doesn't know about them.
   736→		if len(entry.FiringAlerts) > 0 {
   737→			return ReasonAllAlertsResolved
   738→		}
   739→		return ReasonDoNotNotify
   740→	}
   741→
   742→	if n.rs.SendResolved() && !entry.IsResolvedSubset(resolved) {
   743→		return ReasonNewResolvedAlerts
   744→	}
   745→
   746→	// Nothing changed, only notify if the repeat interval has passed.
   747→	isRepeatIntervalElapsed := entry.Timestamp.AsTime().Before(n.now().Add(-repeat))
   748→	if isRepeatIntervalElapsed {
   749→		return ReasonRepeatIntervalElapsed
   750→	}
   751→	return ReasonDoNotNotify
   752→}

    33→// A Muter determines whether a given label set is muted. Implementers that
    34→// maintain an underlying AlertMarker are expected to update it during a call of
    35→// Mutes.
    36→type Muter interface {
    37→	Mutes(ctx context.Context, lset model.LabelSet) bool
    38→}
    39→
    40→// A MuteFunc is a function that implements the Muter interface.
    41→type MuteFunc func(ctx context.Context, lset model.LabelSet) bool
    42→
    43→// Mutes implements the Muter interface.
    44→func (f MuteFunc) Mutes(ctx context.Context, lset model.LabelSet) bool { return f(ctx, lset) }
    45→
    46→// MuteStage filters alerts through a Muter.
    47→type MuteStage struct {
    48→	muter   Muter
    49→	metrics *Metrics
    50→}
    51→
    52→// NewMuteStage return a new MuteStage.
    53→func NewMuteStage(m Muter, metrics *Metrics) *MuteStage {
    54→	return &MuteStage{muter: m, metrics: metrics}
    55→}
    56→
    57→// Exec implements the Stage interface.
    58→func (n *MuteStage) Exec(ctx context.Context, logger *slog.Logger, alerts ...*types.Alert) (context.Context, []*types.Alert, error) {
    59→	ctx, span := tracer.Start(ctx, "notify.MuteStage.Exec",
    60→		trace.WithAttributes(attribute.Int("alerting.alerts.count", len(alerts))),
    61→		trace.WithSpanKind(trace.SpanKindInternal),
    62→	)
    63→	defer span.End()
    64→
    65→	var (
    66→		filtered []*types.Alert
    67→		muted    []*types.Alert
    68→	)
    69→	for _, a := range alerts {
    70→		// TODO(fabxc): increment total alerts counter.
    71→		// Do not send the alert if muted.
    72→		if n.muter.Mutes(ctx, a.Labels) {
    73→			muted = append(muted, a)
    74→		} else {
    75→			filtered = append(filtered, a)
    76→		}
    77→		// TODO(fabxc): increment muted alerts counter if muted.
    78→	}
    79→	if len(muted) > 0 {
    80→
    81→		var reason string
    82→		switch n.muter.(type) {
    83→		case *silence.Silencer:
    84→			reason = SuppressedReasonSilence
    85→		case *inhibit.Inhibitor:
    86→			reason = SuppressedReasonInhibition
    87→		default:
    88→		}
    89→		span.SetAttributes(
    90→			attribute.Int("alerting.alerts.muted.count", len(muted)),
    91→			attribute.Int("alerting.alerts.filtered.count", len(filtered)),
    92→			attribute.String("alerting.suppressed.reason", reason),
    93→		)
    94→		n.metrics.numNotificationSuppressedTotal.WithLabelValues(reason).Add(float64(len(muted)))
    95→		logger.Debug("Notifications will not be sent for muted alerts", "alerts", fmt.Sprintf("%v", muted), "reason", reason)
    96→	}
    97→
    98→	return ctx, filtered, nil
    99→}

   101→// A TimeMuter determines if the time is muted by one or more active or mute
   102→// time intervals. If the time is muted, it returns true and the names of the
   103→// time intervals that muted it. Otherwise, it returns false and a nil slice.
   104→type TimeMuter interface {
   105→	Mutes(timeIntervalNames []string, now time.Time) (bool, []string, error)
   106→}
   107→
   108→type timeStage struct {
   109→	muter   TimeMuter
   110→	marker  types.GroupMarker
   111→	metrics *Metrics
   112→}
   113→
   114→type TimeMuteStage timeStage
   115→
   116→func NewTimeMuteStage(muter TimeMuter, marker types.GroupMarker, metrics *Metrics) *TimeMuteStage {
   117→	return &TimeMuteStage{muter, marker, metrics}
   118→}
   119→
   120→// Exec implements the stage interface for TimeMuteStage.
   121→// TimeMuteStage is responsible for muting alerts whose route is not in an active time.
   122→func (tms TimeMuteStage) Exec(ctx context.Context, l *slog.Logger, alerts ...*types.Alert) (context.Context, []*types.Alert, error) {
   123→	ctx, span := tracer.Start(ctx, "notify.TimeMuteStage.Exec",
   124→		trace.WithAttributes(attribute.Int("alerting.alerts.count", len(alerts))),
   125→		trace.WithSpanKind(trace.SpanKindInternal),
   126→	)
   127→	defer span.End()
   128→
   129→	routeID, ok := RouteID(ctx)
   130→	if !ok {
   131→		err := errors.New("route ID missing")
   132→		span.SetStatus(codes.Error, err.Error())
   133→		span.RecordError(err)
   134→		return ctx, nil, err
   135→	}
   136→	span.SetAttributes(attribute.String("alerting.route.id", routeID))
   137→
   138→	gkey, ok := GroupKey(ctx)
   139→	if !ok {
   140→		return ctx, nil, errors.New("group key missing")
   141→	}
   142→	span.SetAttributes(attribute.String("alerting.group.key", gkey))
   143→
   144→	muteTimeIntervalNames, ok := MuteTimeIntervalNames(ctx)
   145→	if !ok {
   146→		return ctx, alerts, nil
   147→	}
   148→	now, ok := Now(ctx)
   149→	if !ok {
   150→		return ctx, alerts, errors.New("missing now timestamp")
   151→	}
   152→
   153→	// Skip this stage if there are no mute timings.
   154→	if len(muteTimeIntervalNames) == 0 {
   155→		return ctx, alerts, nil
   156→	}
   157→
   158→	muted, mutedBy, err := tms.muter.Mutes(muteTimeIntervalNames, now)
   159→	if err != nil {
   160→		span.SetStatus(codes.Error, err.Error())
   161→		span.RecordError(err)
   162→		return ctx, alerts, err
   163→	}
   164→	// If muted is false then mutedBy is nil and the muted marker is removed.
   165→	tms.marker.SetMuted(routeID, gkey, mutedBy)
   166→
   167→	// If the current time is inside a mute time, all alerts are removed from the pipeline.
   168→	if muted {
   169→		tms.metrics.numNotificationSuppressedTotal.WithLabelValues(SuppressedReasonMuteTimeInterval).Add(float64(len(alerts)))
   170→		l.Debug("Notifications not sent, route is within mute time", "alerts", len(alerts))
   171→		span.AddEvent("notify.TimeMuteStage.Exec muted the alerts")
   172→		return ctx, nil, nil
   173→	}
   174→
   175→	return ctx, alerts, nil
   176→}

   180→func NewTimeActiveStage(muter TimeMuter, marker types.GroupMarker, metrics *Metrics) *TimeActiveStage {

   184→// Exec implements the stage interface for TimeActiveStage.
   185→// TimeActiveStage is responsible for muting alerts whose route is not in an active time.
   186→func (tas TimeActiveStage) Exec(ctx context.Context, l *slog.Logger, alerts ...*types.Alert) (context.Context, []*types.Alert, error) {
   187→	routeID, ok := RouteID(ctx)
   188→	if !ok {
   189→		return ctx, nil, errors.New("route ID missing")
   190→	}
   191→
   192→	ctx, span := tracer.Start(ctx, "notify.TimeActiveStage.Exec",
   193→		trace.WithAttributes(attribute.String("alerting.route.id", routeID)),
   194→		trace.WithAttributes(attribute.Int("alerting.alerts.count", len(alerts))),
   195→		trace.WithSpanKind(trace.SpanKindInternal),
   196→	)
   197→	defer span.End()
   198→
   199→	gkey, ok := GroupKey(ctx)
   200→	if !ok {
   201→		return ctx, nil, errors.New("group key missing")
   202→	}
   203→
   204→	activeTimeIntervalNames, ok := ActiveTimeIntervalNames(ctx)
   205→	if !ok {
   206→		return ctx, alerts, nil
   207→	}
   208→
   209→	// if we don't have active time intervals at all it is always active.
   210→	if len(activeTimeIntervalNames) == 0 {
   211→		return ctx, alerts, nil
   212→	}
   213→
   214→	now, ok := Now(ctx)
   215→	if !ok {
   216→		return ctx, alerts, errors.New("missing now timestamp")
   217→	}
   218→
   219→	active, _, err := tas.muter.Mutes(activeTimeIntervalNames, now)
   220→	if err != nil {
   221→		return ctx, alerts, err
   222→	}
   223→
   224→	var mutedBy []string
   225→	if !active {
   226→		// If the group is muted, then it must be muted by all active time intervals.
   227→		// Otherwise, the group must be in at least one active time interval for it
   228→		// to be active.
   229→		mutedBy = activeTimeIntervalNames
   230→	}
   231→	tas.marker.SetMuted(routeID, gkey, mutedBy)
   232→
   233→	// If the current time is not inside an active time, all alerts are removed from the pipeline
   234→	if !active {
   235→		span.AddEvent("notify.TimeActiveStage.Exec not active, removing all alerts")
   236→		tas.metrics.numNotificationSuppressedTotal.WithLabelValues(SuppressedReasonActiveTimeInterval).Add(float64(len(alerts)))
   237→		l.Debug("Notifications not sent, route is not within active time", "alerts", len(alerts))
   238→		return ctx, nil, nil
   239→	}
   240→
   241→	return ctx, alerts, nil
   242→}

Implementers that\n// maintain an underlying AlertMarker are expected to update it during a call of\n// Mutes.\ntype Muter interface {\n\tMutes(ctx context.Context, lset model.LabelSet) bool\n}\n\n// A MuteFunc is a function that implements the Muter interface.\ntype MuteFunc func(ctx context.Context, lset model.LabelSet) bool\n\n// Mutes implements the Muter interface.\nfunc (f MuteFunc) Mutes(ctx context.Context, lset model.LabelSet) bool { return f(ctx, lset) }\n\n// MuteStage filters alerts through a Muter.\ntype MuteStage struct {\n\tmuter   Muter\n\tmetrics *Metrics\n}\n\n// NewMuteStage return a new MuteStage.\nfunc NewMuteStage(m Muter, metrics *Metrics) *MuteStage {\n\treturn &MuteStage{muter: m, metrics: metrics}\n}\n\n// Exec implements the Stage interface.\nfunc (n *MuteStage) Exec(ctx context.Context, logger *slog.Logger, alerts ...*types.Alert) (context.Context, []*types.Alert, error) {\n\tctx, span := tracer.Start(ctx, \"notify.MuteStage.Exec\",\n\t\ttrace.WithAttributes(attribute.Int(\"alerting.alerts.count\", len(alerts))),\n\t\ttrace.WithSpanKind(trace.SpanKindInternal),\n\t)\n\tdefer span.End()\n\n\tvar (\n\t\tfiltered []*types.Alert\n\t\tmuted    []*types.Alert\n\t)\n\tfor _, a := range alerts {\n\t\t// TODO(fabxc): increment total alerts counter.\n\t\t// Do not send the alert if muted.\n\t\tif n.muter.Mutes(ctx, a.Labels) {\n\t\t\tmuted = append(muted, a)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tfiltered = append(filtered, a)\n\t\t}\n\t\t// TODO(fabxc): increment muted alerts counter if muted.\n\t}\n\tif len(muted) > 0 {\n\n\t\tvar reason string\n\t\tswitch n.muter.(type) {\n\t\tcase *silence.Silencer:\n\t\t\treason = SuppressedReasonSilence\n\t\tcase *inhibit.Inhibitor:\n\t\t\treason = SuppressedReasonInhibition\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t}\n\t\tspan.SetAttributes(\n\t\t\tattribute.Int(\"alerting.alerts.muted.count\", len(muted)),\n\t\t\tattribute.Int(\"alerting.alerts.filtered.count\", len(filtered)),\n\t\t\tattribute.String(\"alerting.suppressed.reason\", reason),\n\t\t)\n\t\tn.metrics.numNotificationSuppressedTotal.WithLabelValues(reason).Add(float64(len(muted)))\n\t\tlogger.Debug(\"Notifications will not be sent for muted alerts\", \"alerts\", fmt.Sprintf(\"%v\", muted), \"reason\", reason)\n\t}\n\n\treturn ctx, filtered, nil\n}\n\n// A TimeMuter determines if the time is muted by one or more active or mute\n// time intervals. If the time is muted, it returns true and the names of the\n// time intervals that muted it. Otherwise, it returns false and a nil slice.\ntype TimeMuter interface {\n\tMutes(timeIntervalNames []string, now time.Time) (bool, []string, error)\n}\n\ntype timeStage struct {\n\tmuter   TimeMuter\n\tmarker  types.GroupMarker\n\tmetrics *Metrics\n}\n\ntype TimeMuteStage timeStage\n\nfunc NewTimeMuteStage(muter TimeMuter, marker types.GroupMarker, metrics *Metrics) *TimeMuteStage {\n\treturn &TimeMuteStage{muter, marker, metrics}\n}\n\n// Exec implements the stage interface for TimeMuteStage.\n// TimeMuteStage is responsible for muting alerts whose route is not in an active time.\nfunc (tms TimeMuteStage) Exec(ctx context.Context, l *slog.Logger, alerts ...*types.Alert) (context.Context, []*types.Alert, error) {\n\tctx, span := tracer.Start(ctx, \"notify.TimeMuteStage.Exec\",\n\t\ttrace.WithAttributes(attribute.Int(\"alerting.alerts.count\", len(alerts))),\n\t\ttrace.WithSpanKind(trace.SpanKindInternal),\n\t)\n\tdefer span.End()\n\n\trouteID, ok := RouteID(ctx)\n\tif !ok {\n\t\terr := errors.New(\"route ID missing\")\n\t\tspan.SetStatus(codes.Error, err.Error())\n\t\tspan.RecordError(err)\n\t\treturn ctx, nil, err\n\t}\n\tspan.SetAttributes(attribute.String(\"alerting.route.id\", routeID))\n\n\tgkey, ok := GroupKey(ctx)\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn ctx, nil, errors.New(\"group key missing\")\n\t}\n\tspan.SetAttributes(attribute.String(\"alerting.group.key\", gkey))\n\n\tmuteTimeIntervalNames, ok := MuteTimeIntervalNames(ctx)\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn ctx, alerts, nil\n\t}\n\tnow, ok := Now(ctx)\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn ctx, alerts, errors.New(\"missing now timestamp\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Skip this stage if there are no mute timings.\n\tif len(muteTimeIntervalNames) == 0 {\n\t\treturn ctx, alerts, nil\n\t}\n\n\tmuted, mutedBy, err := tms.muter.Mutes(muteTimeIntervalNames, now)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tspan.SetStatus(codes.Error, err.Error())\n\t\tspan.RecordError(err)\n\t\treturn ctx, alerts, err\n\t}\n\t// If muted is false then mutedBy is nil and the muted marker is removed.\n\ttms.marker.SetMuted(routeID, gkey, mutedBy)\n\n\t// If the current time is inside a mute time, all alerts are removed from the pipeline.\n\tif muted {\n\t\ttms.metrics.numNotificationSuppressedTotal.WithLabelValues(SuppressedReasonMuteTimeInterval).Add(float64(len(alerts)))\n\t\tl.Debug(\"Notifications not sent, route is within mute time\", \"alerts\", len(alerts))\n\t\tspan.AddEvent(\"notify.TimeMuteStage.Exec muted the alerts\")\n\t\treturn ctx, nil, nil\n\t}\n\n\treturn ctx, alerts, nil\n}\n\ntype TimeActiveStage timeStage\n\nfunc NewTimeActiveStage(muter TimeMuter, marker types.GroupMarker, metrics *Metrics) *TimeActiveStage {\n\treturn &TimeActiveStage{muter, marker, metrics}\n}\n\n// Exec implements the stage interface for TimeActiveStage.\n// TimeActiveStage is responsible for muting alerts whose route is not in an active time.\nfunc (tas TimeActiveStage) Exec(ctx context.Context, l *slog.Logger, alerts ...*types.Alert) (context.Context, []*types.Alert, error) {\n\trouteID, ok := RouteID(ctx)\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn ctx, nil, errors.New(\"route ID missing\")\n\t}\n\n\tctx, span := tracer.Start(ctx, \"notify.TimeActiveStage.Exec\",\n\t\ttrace.WithAttributes(attribute.String(\"alerting.route.id\", routeID)),\n\t\ttrace.WithAttributes(attribute.Int(\"alerting.alerts.count\", len(alerts))),\n\t\ttrace.WithSpanKind(trace.SpanKindInternal),\n\t)\n\tdefer span.End()\n\n\tgkey, ok := GroupKey(ctx)\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn ctx, nil, errors.New(\"group key missing\")\n\t}\n\n\tactiveTimeIntervalNames, ok := ActiveTimeIntervalNames(ctx)\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn ctx, alerts, nil\n\t}\n\n\t// if we don't have active time intervals at all it is always active.\n\tif len(activeTimeIntervalNames) == 0 {\n\t\treturn ctx, alerts, nil\n\t}\n\n\tnow, ok := Now(ctx)\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn ctx, alerts, errors.New(\"missing now timestamp\")\n\t}\n\n\tactive, _, err := tas.muter.Mutes(activeTimeIntervalNames, now)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn ctx, alerts, err\n\t}\n\n\tvar mutedBy []string\n\tif !active {\n\t\t// If the group is muted, then it must be muted by all active time intervals.\n\t\t// Otherwise, the group must be in at least one active time interval for it\n\t\t// to be active.\n\t\tmutedBy = activeTimeIntervalNames\n\t}\n\ttas.marker.SetMuted(routeID, gkey, mutedBy)\n\n\t// If the current time is not inside an active time, all alerts are removed from the pipeline\n\tif !active {\n\t\tspan.AddEvent(\"notify.TimeActiveStage.Exec not active, removing all alerts\")\n\t\ttas.metrics.numNotificationSuppressedTotal.WithLabelValues(SuppressedReasonActiveTimeInterval).Add(float64(len(alerts)))\n\t\tl.Debug(\"Notifications not sent, route is not within active time\", \"alerts\", len(alerts))\n\t\treturn ctx, nil, nil\n\t}\n\n\treturn ctx, alerts, nil\n}\n", "numLines": 243, "startLine": 1, "totalLines": 243}}

    42→// API represents all APIs of Alertmanager.
    43→type API struct {
    44→	v2                *apiv2.API
    45→	deprecationRouter *V1DeprecationRouter
    46→
    47→	requestDuration          *prometheus.HistogramVec
    48→	requestsInFlight         prometheus.Gauge
    49→	concurrencyLimitExceeded prometheus.Counter
    50→	timeout                  time.Duration
    51→	inFlightSem              chan struct{}
    52→}
    53→
    54→// Options for the creation of an API object. Alerts, Silences, AlertStatusFunc
    55→// and GroupMutedFunc are mandatory. The zero value for everything else is a safe
    56→// default.
    57→type Options struct {
    58→	// Alerts to be used by the API. Mandatory.
    59→	Alerts provider.Alerts
    60→	// Silences to be used by the API. Mandatory.
    61→	Silences *silence.Silences
    62→	// AlertStatusFunc is used be the API to retrieve the AlertStatus of an
    63→	// alert. Mandatory.
    64→	AlertStatusFunc func(model.Fingerprint) types.AlertStatus
    65→	// GroupMutedFunc is used be the API to know if an alert is muted.
    66→	// Mandatory.
    67→	GroupMutedFunc func(routeID, groupKey string) ([]string, bool)
    68→	// Peer from the gossip cluster. If nil, no clustering will be used.
    69→	Peer cluster.ClusterPeer
    70→	// Timeout for all HTTP connections. The zero value (and negative
    71→	// values) result in no timeout.
    72→	Timeout time.Duration
    73→	// Concurrency limit for GET requests. The zero value (and negative
    74→	// values) result in a limit of GOMAXPROCS or 8, whichever is
    75→	// larger. Status code 503 is served for GET requests that would exceed
    76→	// the concurrency limit.
    77→	Concurrency int
    78→	// Logger is used for logging, if nil, no logging will happen.
    79→	Logger *slog.Logger
    80→	// Registry is used to register Prometheus metrics. If nil, no metrics
    81→	// registration will happen.
    82→	Registry prometheus.Registerer
    83→	// RequestDuration is used to measure the duration of HTTP requests.
    84→	RequestDuration *prometheus.HistogramVec
    85→	// GroupFunc returns a list of alert groups. The alerts are grouped
    86→	// according to the current active configuration. Alerts returned are
    87→	// filtered by the arguments provided to the function.
    88→	GroupFunc func(context.Context, func(*dispatch.Route) bool, func(*types.Alert, time.Time) bool) (dispatch.AlertGroups, map[model.Fingerprint][]string, error)
    89→}
    90→
    91→func (o Options) validate() error {
    92→	if o.Alerts == nil {
    93→		return errors.New("mandatory field Alerts not set")
    94→	}
    95→	if o.Silences == nil {
    96→		return errors.New("mandatory field Silences not set")
    97→	}
    98→	if o.AlertStatusFunc == nil {
    99→		return errors.New("mandatory field AlertStatusFunc not set")
   100→	}

443→func (d *Dispatcher) groupAlert(ctx context.Context, alert *types.Alert, route *Route) {
   444→	_, span := tracer.Start(ctx, "dispatch.Dispatcher.groupAlert",
   445→		trace.WithAttributes(
   446→			attribute.String("alerting.alert.name", alert.Name()),
   447→			attribute.String("alerting.alert.fingerprint", alert.Fingerprint().String()),
   448→			attribute.String("alerting.route.receiver.name", route.RouteOpts.Receiver),
   449→		),
   450→		trace.WithSpanKind(trace.SpanKindInternal),
   451→	)
   452→	defer span.End()
   453→
   454→	now := time.Now()
   455→	groupLabels := getGroupLabels(alert, route)
   456→
   457→	fp := groupLabels.Fingerprint()
   458→
   459→	el, loaded := d.routeGroupsSlice[route.Idx].groups.Load(fp)
   460→	if loaded {
   461→		ag := el.(*aggrGroup)
   462→		// Try to insert into the aggrgroup.
   463→		// If it's destroyed insert will return false.
   464→		if ag.insert(ctx, alert) {
   465→			return
   466→		}
   467→	}
   468→
   469→	// If we couldn't insert, we need to create a new aggregation group.
   470→	// Since multiple goroutines might be trying to create the same group concurrently
   471→	// we will use the sync map swap to ensure only one of them creates it.
   472→
   473→	// If the group does not exist, create it. But check the limit first.
   474→	limit := d.limits.MaxNumberOfAggregationGroups()
   475→	current := int(d.aggrGroupsNum.Load())
   476→	if limit > 0 && current >= limit {
   477→		d.metrics.aggrGroupLimitReached.Inc()
   478→		err := errors.New("too many aggregation groups, cannot create new group for alert")
   479→		message := "Failed to create aggregation group"
   480→		d.logger.Error(message, "err", err.Error(), "groups", current, "limit", limit, "alert", alert.Name())
   481→		span.SetStatus(codes.Error, message)
   482→		span.RecordError(err,
   483→			trace.WithAttributes(
   484→				attribute.Int("alerting.aggregation_group.count", current),
   485→				attribute.Int("alerting.aggregation_group.limit", limit),
   486→			),
   487→		)
   488→		return
   489→	}
   490→
   491→	ag := newAggrGroup(d.ctx, groupLabels, route, d.timeout, d.marker.(types.AlertMarker), d.logger)
   492→	// Insert the 1st alert in the group before starting the group's run()
   493→	// function, to make sure that when the run() will be executed the 1st
   494→	// alert is already there.
   495→	ag.insert(ctx, alert)
   496→
   497→	retries := 0
   498→	for {
   499→		if loaded {

  "text": "The Prometheus Alertmanager notification pipeline flows from alert ingestion through routing, inhibition and silence checks, to final notifier dispatch:\n\n1. ALERT INGESTION (api/v2/api.go:postAlertsHandler): HTTP POST requests to /api/v2/alerts are received by postAlertsHandler which validates alerts and calls api.alerts.Put() to store them in the alert provider.\n\n2. ALERT STORAGE (provider/mem/mem.go:Alerts.Put): The in-memory alert provider stores alerts in its internal store and notifies subscribers via channels. This is where external systems (like Prometheus) inject alerts.\n\n3. ALERT PROCESSING IN DISPATCHER (dispatch/dispatch.go:Dispatcher.run): The dispatcher subscribes to the alert provider and receives new/updated alerts through an iterator. Multiple alert ingestion workers process these concurrently, calling routeAlert for each alert.\n\n4. ROUTING TREE TRAVERSAL (dispatch/dispatch.go:Dispatcher.routeAlert and dispatch/route.go:Route.Match): For each alert, routeAlert calls route.Match(alert.Labels) to perform depth-first left-to-right traversal of the routing tree. Route.Match recursively checks matchers against alert labels to find all matching routes, respecting the Continue flag to determine if sibling routes should also be checked.\n\n5. AGGREGATION GROUPING (dispatch/dispatch.go:Dispatcher.groupAlert): For each matched route, the dispatcher groups the alert by computing group labels based on the route's GroupBy configuration. Alerts with the same group labels are placed in the same aggregation group, which applies GroupWait/GroupInterval timers.\n\n6. NOTIFICATION PIPELINE EXECUTION (dispatch/dispatch.go:Dispatcher.runAG): Each aggregation group executes the notification pipeline via d.stage.Exec(). The stage is a RoutingStage that routes to the appropriate receiver's MultiStage pipeline.\n\n7. INHIBITION CHECK (notify/mute.go:MuteStage.Exec and inhibit/inhibit.go:Inhibitor.Mutes): The inhibition stage uses the Inhibitor to check if alerts match inhibition rules. For each alert, Inhibitor.Mutes checks if any inhibition rule's target matchers match and if source alerts satisfying the Equal conditions exist. If an alert is inhibited, it's filtered from the pipeline.\n\n8. SILENCE CHECK (notify/mute.go:MuteStage.Exec and silence/silence.go:Silencer.Mutes): The silence stage uses the Silencer to check if alerts match active or pending silences. Silencer.Mutes queries the silence store and compares silence matchers against alert labels, with caching to optimize repeated checks.\n\n9. FINAL NOTIFIER DISPATCH (notify/notify.go:RetryStage.Exec): After passing through muting stages, remaining alerts reach the RetryStage which invokes the actual notifier integration (email, Slack, PagerDuty, etc.). The RetryStage implements exponential backoff retry logic and records notification metrics. Each integration is wrapped in a FanoutStage that executes all integrations for a receiver concurrently.\n\nThe complete pipeline is built by PipelineBuilder.New (notify/notify.go:PipelineBuilder.New) which constructs a MultiStage pipeline containing: GossipSettleStage (cluster coordination) → MuteStage (inhibition) → TimeActiveStage (active time intervals) → TimeMuteStage (mute time intervals) → MuteStage (silence) → FanoutStage of receivers, where each receiver contains WaitStage → DedupStage → RetryStage → SetNotifiesStage."
}

  "text": "The Prometheus Alertmanager notification pipeline flows from alert ingestion through routing, inhibition and silence checks, to final notifier dispatch:\n\n1. ALERT INGESTION (api/v2/api.go:postAlertsHandler): HTTP POST requests to /api/v2/alerts are received by postAlertsHandler which validates alerts and calls api.alerts.Put() to store them in the alert provider.\n\n2. ALERT STORAGE (provider/mem/mem.go:Alerts.Put): The in-memory alert provider stores alerts in its internal store and notifies subscribers via channels. This is where external systems (like Prometheus) inject alerts.\n\n3. ALERT PROCESSING IN DISPATCHER (dispatch/dispatch.go:Dispatcher.run): The dispatcher subscribes to the alert provider and receives new/updated alerts through an iterator. Multiple alert ingestion workers process these concurrently, calling routeAlert for each alert.\n\n4. ROUTING TREE TRAVERSAL (dispatch/dispatch.go:Dispatcher.routeAlert and dispatch/route.go:Route.Match): For each alert, routeAlert calls route.Match(alert.Labels) to perform depth-first left-to-right traversal of the routing tree. Route.Match recursively checks matchers against alert labels to find all matching routes, respecting the Continue flag to determine if sibling routes should also be checked.\n\n5. AGGREGATION GROUPING (dispatch/dispatch.go:Dispatcher.groupAlert): For each matched route, the dispatcher groups the alert by computing group labels based on the route's GroupBy configuration. Alerts with the same group labels are placed in the same aggregation group, which applies GroupWait/GroupInterval timers.\n\n6. NOTIFICATION PIPELINE EXECUTION (dispatch/dispatch.go:Dispatcher.runAG): Each aggregation group executes the notification pipeline via d.stage.Exec(). The stage is a RoutingStage that routes to the appropriate receiver's MultiStage pipeline.\n\n7. INHIBITION CHECK (notify/mute.go:MuteStage.Exec and inhibit/inhibit.go:Inhibitor.Mutes): The inhibition stage uses the Inhibitor to check if alerts match inhibition rules. For each alert, Inhibitor.Mutes checks if any inhibition rule's target matchers match and if source alerts satisfying the Equal conditions exist. If an alert is inhibited, it's filtered from the pipeline.\n\n8. SILENCE CHECK (notify/mute.go:MuteStage.Exec and silence/silence.go:Silencer.Mutes): The silence stage uses the Silencer to check if alerts match active or pending silences. Silencer.Mutes queries the silence store and compares silence matchers against alert labels, with caching to optimize repeated checks.\n\n9. FINAL NOTIFIER DISPATCH (notify/notify.go:RetryStage.Exec): After passing through muting stages, remaining alerts reach the RetryStage which invokes the actual notifier integration (email, Slack, PagerDuty, etc.). The RetryStage implements exponential backoff retry logic and records notification metrics. Each integration is wrapped in a FanoutStage that executes all integrations for a receiver concurrently.\n\nThe complete pipeline is built by PipelineBuilder.New (notify/notify.go:PipelineBuilder.New) which constructs a MultiStage pipeline containing: GossipSettleStage (cluster coordination) → MuteStage (inhibition) → TimeActiveStage (active time intervals) → TimeMuteStage (mute time intervals) → MuteStage (silence) → FanoutStage of receivers, where each receiver contains WaitStage → DedupStage → RetryStage → SetNotifiesStage."
